title: oxalis kalbreyeri
intro: Oxalis kalbreyeri is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae), commonly found in the Andean region, known for its unique, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant contains oxalic acid and other compounds, which have been reported to exhibit medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Oxalis kalbreyeri are believed to include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, with the leaves and flowers often being used in herbal remedies.
